name: From Cortegana to Castaño del Robledo – circular via Almonaster La Real
name_original: Desde Cortegana a Castaño del Robledo – circular por Almonaster La Real
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Cortegana traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, extensive forests of holm oaks and chestnut trees, and gently flowing streams within the Sierra de Aracena y los Picos de Aroche Natural Park. The region is part of the Sierra Morena mountain range, featuring geology of slate, sandstone, and granite. The Chanza River originates here, contributing to the area's natural features. Best touring cycling routes around Cortegana The most popular touring cycling route is From Cortegana to Castaño del Robledo – circular via Almonaster La Real, a 23.2 miles (37.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 42 minutes to complete. This difficult route connects Cortegana with Almonaster La Real, traversing varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Cerro de San Cristóbal – Linares de la Sierra Square loop from Almonaster - Cortegana, a difficult 48.2 miles (77.5 km) path. It features significant elevation gains through the Sierra Morena foothills. Local touring cyclists also love the Cortegana Castle – Mosque of Almonaster la Real loop from Almonaster la Real, a 28.0 miles (45.0 km) trail leading through historic towns and natural park scenery, often completed in about 3 hours 3 minutes. Touring cycling around Cortegana is defined by rolling hills, holm oak and chestnut forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Cortegana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from 3 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cortegana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Cortegana?
A: Cortegana offers a diverse selection of over 30 touring cycling routes. These routes traverse the varied landscapes of the Sierra de Aracena y los Picos de Aroche Natural Park, providing options for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes near Cortegana?
A: The terrain around Cortegana is characterized by rolling hills, extensive forests of holm oaks and chestnut trees, and gently flowing streams. You'll encounter paths winding through dehesas, eucalyptus and pine plantations, ancient chestnut groves, and river valleys, with geology comprising slate, sandstone, and granite.

Q: How challenging are the touring cycling routes in Cortegana?
A: The routes in Cortegana range from moderate to difficult, with a significant number of routes featuring considerable elevation gains. There are 12 moderate routes and 22 difficult routes, ensuring options for both intermediate and experienced cyclists.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Cortegana?
A: The Sierra de Aracena y los Picos de Aroche Natural Park, where Cortegana is located, generally offers pleasant conditions for cycling during spring and autumn. These seasons provide milder temperatures and lush landscapes, making for a more enjoyable experience than the hotter summer months.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or natural features to see along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the routes often pass by significant landmarks and natural features. You can explore the medieval &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5300310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cortegana Castle&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views, or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4550593" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mosque of Almonaster la Real&lt;/a&gt;. Natural highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4086077" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de San Cristóbal&lt;/a&gt; and the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7287170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall in the Sierra de Aracena and Picos de Aroche Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find viewpoints with scenic vistas on the touring cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The hilly terrain and natural beauty of the region provide numerous opportunities for scenic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2894628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alájar Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is a notable spot, and many routes, such as the one passing by &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4086077" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de San Cristóbal&lt;/a&gt;, offer expansive views of the surrounding natural park.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in Cortegana?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Cortegana are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987198426/from-cortegana-to-castano-del-robledo-circular-via-almonaster-la-real" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Cortegana to Castaño del Robledo – circular via Almonaster La Real&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a comprehensive tour of the local landscape.

Q: Are there any advanced touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Experienced cyclists will find plenty of challenging options. For instance, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122578" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de San Cristóbal – Linares de la Sierra Square loop from Almonaster - Cortegana&lt;/a&gt; covers 77.5 km with significant elevation gains, providing a demanding ride through the Sierra Morena foothills.

Q: Can you recommend a moderate touring cycling route?
A: For a moderate challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20121878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castaño del Robledo – Galaroza Historic Town Center loop from Jabugo&lt;/a&gt;. This 22 km route offers a pleasant ride with manageable elevation, allowing you to explore charming towns and natural scenery.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Cortegana?
A: The touring cycling routes around Cortegana are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from holm oak and chestnut forests to river valleys, and the blend of natural beauty with historical towns like Almonaster la Real.

Q: Are the touring cycling routes around Cortegana suitable for cycling with dogs?
A: While the natural park setting is generally dog-friendly, it's important to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock. Always check specific route regulations and ensure your dog is fit for the distance and terrain, as many routes involve significant climbs and descents.

Q: Where can I find cafes or places to rest along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes connect Cortegana with nearby towns such as Almonaster la Real and Jabugo, where you can find cafes, restaurants, and local shops to rest and refuel. The charming villages within the natural park are excellent spots for a break.

Q: Are there parking facilities near the touring cycling routes?
A: Cortegana and the surrounding towns typically offer public parking areas. When planning your route, it's advisable to check the starting point for available parking options, especially in smaller villages, to ensure convenient access to the trails.
tips: Before entering Cortegana, it is worth stopping at this viewpoint to enjoy the fantastic views of the town.

In recent decades this medieval monument has been appreciated and with good reason! It is the old Muslim mosque, later converted into the Christian Hermitage of the Conception of Our Lady. There are records of its existence for more than 1,700 years, and it is supposed to have been built on top of some monumental Roman building from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

The Castaño del Robledo farmhouses have been declared a Historic-Artistic Site. The oldest farmhouse is in the vicinity of El Álamo square, where the Church of Santiago is also located, which is a very characteristic point of the town.
